title: Hieracium dmitrovense
taxonomy: Hieracium dmitrovense is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as dandelions and related species. It belongs to the genus Hieracium, which consists of hawkweeds. The exact classification of Hieracium dmitrovense within the Hieracium genus is not well-documented in readily available sources. Further research would be required to determine its exact classification within the genus. It is a rare and lesser-known species of plant.

intro: Hieracium dmitrovense is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae), commonly known as hawkweed, which is characterized by its yellow or white flowers and bitter leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium dmitrovense include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Hieracium dmitrovense is often prepared as a tincture or infused in tea, and it is also used in some pharmaceutical formulations, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities.
